CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-01-2017 Set For Trial On Oct-10-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Special Motion To Strike Complaint Of Plaintiff Jay Hung
Off Calendar Jan-06-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JANET M. BRAYER, TEL. NO. 1-415-445-9555 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. RYAN W. STAHL OF THE SCHERE SMITH & KENNY LLP, TEL. NO. 1-415-433-1099 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendants WebMocha, LLC and George Diaz's anti-SLAPP motion is denied as untimely. Defendants acknowledge that they filed their motion more than sixty days after service of the complaint on them. The court exercises its discretion per CCP 425.16(f) not to permit this late filed motion. "In determining whether to permit a late motion, the most important consideration is whether the filing advances the anti-SLAPP statute's purpose of examining the merits of covered lawsuits in the early stages of the proceedings." (San Diegans for Open Government v. Har Construction, Inc. (2015) 240 Cal.App.4th 611, 624.) Adjudication of the motion on its merits does not advance the purpose of the anti-SLAPP statute since the motion is only directed to two of six causes of action alleged by plaintiff Jay Hung and those causes of action appear to be subsidiary to some or all of the other causes of action alleged by Mr. Hung. Thus, even if the motion were granted, the majority of Mr. Hung's claims would still need to be adjudicated. Moreover, Defendants' motion focuses on only two documents (the McGrane email and the Statement of Information) out of what is likely to be numerous documents relating, referring to and memorializing the parties' business relationship and thus eliminating those two documents as any bases for Mr. Hung's claims (the alternative relief sought by Defendants' motion) has minimal consequence for the ultimate resolution of this case. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/hk
